source.title: 2018 AHA/ACC Guideline for the Management of Adults With Congenital Heart Disease
source.organization: American College of Cardiology/American Heart Association

signals: 2018 AHA/ACC Guideline for the Management of Adults
Management of Adults With Congenital Heart Disease
Task Force on Clinical Practice Guidelines

extraction.summary: This is the 2018 AHA/ACC guideline for management of adults with congenital heart disease (ACHD). It addresses the growing population of ACHD patients who survived to adulthood but require lifelong specialized cardiac care due to ongoing sequelae of their congenital defects.

population.risk_criteria: Patients with congenital heart disease surviving to adulthood
population.disease_definition: Structural cardiac abnormalities present before birth attributable to abnormal fetal cardiac development
population.exclusions: Children (<18 years), inherited disorders with cardiac manifestations (Marfan syndrome, hypertrophic cardiomyopathy), anatomic variants (patent foramen ovale)
population.severity_staging: Heterogeneous population with varying severity based on underlying anatomy, physiology, and surgical repair/palliation status

key_thresholds.context: Maximum diameter for mild aortic enlargement
Maximum diameter for moderate aortic enlargement
Maximum diameter for severe aortic enlargement
Oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry at rest
Oxygen saturation at rest
Mean PA pressure by right heart catheterization
Sustained Qp:Qs ratio
threshold for shunt closure consideration
pulmonary-to-systemic blood flow ratio threshold
Functional capacity assessment in symptomatic patients
Severe systemic ventricular dysfunction - high risk for pregnancy
Upper limit for safe ASD closure
Threshold for cyanosis detection with exercise
Iron deficiency treatment threshold in cyanotic patients
Level requiring adjusted coagulation testing
Threshold for surgical intervention
Surgical intervention indication
Mild severity progressive aortic stenosis
Turner syndrome aortic root replacement threshold
Significant native or recurrent coarctation
Severe pulmonary stenosis
Peak velocity <3 m/s
Peak velocity 3-4 m/s
Peak velocity >4 m/s, mean gradient >35 mmHg
Severe RV dilation threshold for pulmonary valve replacement in TOF
Severe RV dilation threshold for pulmonary valve replacement in TOF
Risk factor for sudden cardiac death in TOF
RVOT obstruction threshold for intervention
10-year postoperative survival threshold for CCTGA tricuspid valve replacement

recommendations.recommendation: ACHD patients require specialized care in ACHD programs with multidisciplinary expertise
recommendations.notes: Based on heterogeneity of population and complexity of care needs

recommendations.recommendation: Guidelines should be used in conjunction with clinical judgment and patient preferences
recommendations.notes: Guidelines intended to define practices for most but not all circumstances

recommendations.recommendation: Physicians caring for patients with ACHD should support access to care by assuring smooth transitions for adolescents and young adults from pediatric to adult providers and promoting awareness of the need for lifelong specialized care through outreach and educational programs
recommendations.notes: Patients with ACHD have better outcomes when cared for in ACHD centers with specialized expertise

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with ACHD AP classification IB-D, IIA-D, and IIIA-D should be managed in collaboration with an ACHD cardiologist
recommendations.notes: Specialized centers show lower mortality than non-specialized care

recommendations.recommendation: Cardiac surgery, catheter-based interventional procedures, and electrophysiological procedures involving congenital heart lesions should be performed by operators with CHD expertise
recommendations.notes: Better outcomes in specialized ACHD centers

recommendations.recommendation: Standard 12-lead ECG is recommended in adults with CHD with serial assessment depending on ACHD AP classification
recommendations.notes: Essential for baseline comparison and follow-up

recommendations.recommendation: Strategies to limit and monitor radiation exposure are recommended during imaging
recommendations.notes: Use non-ionizing radiation studies when appropriate

recommendations.recommendation: Serial CMR recommended for quantitative assessment of RV size and function in patients with or at risk of RV enlargement/dysfunction
recommendations.notes: Provides reproducible and reliable data compared to alternative techniques

recommendations.recommendation: CMR can be useful in initial evaluation and serial assessment of selected CHD patients based on anatomic complexity and clinical status
recommendations.notes: Particularly valuable for extracardiac cardiovascular defects

recommendations.recommendation: CCT imaging can be useful when information cannot be obtained by other diagnostic modalities
recommendations.notes: Must justify exposure to ionizing radiation

recommendations.recommendation: Cardiac catheterization should be performed by or in collaboration with cardiologists with ACHD expertise
recommendations.notes: For AP classification II-III or interventional procedures

recommendations.recommendation: CPET can be useful for baseline functional assessment and serial testing
recommendations.notes: More precise than patient history for functional evaluation

recommendations.recommendation: Developmentally appropriate transition education should be delivered to adolescent CHD patients
recommendations.notes: Should start in early adolescence and continue beyond 18 years

recommendations.recommendation: Assess activity levels regularly and counsel about appropriate exercise types and intensity
recommendations.notes: Most ACHD patients can safely engage in regular moderate physical activity

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with ACHD should be evaluated for depression and anxiety
recommendations.notes: Mental health issues are common and underrecognized

recommendations.recommendation: Genetic testing for 22q11 deletions is reasonable for patients with conotruncal cardiac defects
recommendations.notes: Up to 5% of children with CHD have DiGeorge syndrome

recommendations.recommendation: Patients at risk for hepatitis C should be screened and vaccinated
recommendations.notes: Risk due to blood exposure during cardiac surgery

recommendations.recommendation: Noncardiac surgery for complex ACHD should be performed with ACHD expert consultation
recommendations.notes: For AP classification IB-D, IIA-D, and IIIA-D

recommendations.recommendation: Patients with ACHD at risk for hepatitis C should be screened and vaccinated for viral hepatitis and treated as appropriate
recommendations.notes: Particularly important for those with blood exposure before 1992

recommendations.recommendation: Optimization before and close surveillance after invasive procedures is beneficial for patients with ACHD
recommendations.notes: Regardless of anatomic defect complexity or procedure type

recommendations.recommendation: Noncardiac surgical procedures should be performed in a hospital with ACHD expertise for complex patients
recommendations.notes: For ACHD AP classification IB-D, IIA-D, and IIIA-D patients

recommendations.recommendation: Women with CHD should receive prepregnancy counseling with ACHD cardiologist input
recommendations.notes: To determine maternal cardiac, obstetrical and fetal risks

recommendations.recommendation: High-risk women should be counseled against pregnancy or given termination option
recommendations.notes: Includes PAH, Eisenmenger syndrome, severe ventricular dysfunction

recommendations.recommendation: Discussion of end-of-life issues and advance directives can be beneficial for patients with ACHD or their surrogates
recommendations.notes: Early discussion favored by patients, timing should be individualized

recommendations.recommendation: Pulse oximetry at rest and during exercise is recommended for evaluation of adults with unrepaired or repaired ASD with residual shunt
recommendations.notes: To determine direction and magnitude of shunt

recommendations.recommendation: Echocardiographic imaging is recommended to guide percutaneous ASD closure
recommendations.notes: Standard of care for guidance during closure procedures

recommendations.recommendation: Transcatheter or surgical closure for secundum ASD causing functional impairment with significant shunt
recommendations.notes: When Qp:Qs ≥1.5:1, PA pressure <50% systemic, PVR <1/3 systemic

recommendations.recommendation: ASD closure should not be performed with PA systolic pressure >2/3 systemic, PVR >2/3 systemic, or net right-to-left shunt
recommendations.notes: Harm classification due to contraindications

recommendations.recommendation: ASD closure recommended for hemodynamically significant secundum ASD with moderate or large left-to-right shunt and RV volume overload in absence of significant PAH
recommendations.notes: Patients benefit from surgical or transcatheter closure

recommendations.recommendation: CMR or CTA recommended for evaluation of partial anomalous pulmonary venous connection
recommendations.notes: Superior to echocardiography for extracardiac vascular anatomy

recommendations.recommendation: VSD closure should not be performed in adults with severe PAH
recommendations.notes: When PA systolic pressure >2/3 systemic or net right-to-left shunt present

recommendations.recommendation: PDA closure is recommended if left atrial or LV enlargement is present with net left-to-right shunt, PA systolic pressure <50% systemic and pulmonary vascular resistance <1/3 systemic
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Surgical intervention is recommended for adults with subAS, maximum gradient ≥50 mmHg and symptoms attributable to subAS
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Adults with bicuspid aortic valve should be evaluated for coarctation of the aorta
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: PDA closure should not be performed in adults with net right-to-left shunt and PA systolic pressure >2/3 systemic
recommendations.notes: Class III harm recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Prophylactic replacement of the aortic root or ascending aorta in adults with Turner syndrome is reasonable when the aortic diameter is 2.5 cm/m² or greater
recommendations.notes: Class IIa recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Surgical repair or catheter-based stenting is recommended for adults with hypertension and significant native or recurrent coarctation of the aorta
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: In adults with moderate or severe valvular pulmonary stenosis and otherwise unexplained symptoms of HF, cyanosis from interatrial right-to-left communication, and/or exercise intolerance, balloon valvuloplasty is recommended
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: In asymptomatic adults with severe valvular pulmonary stenosis, intervention is reasonable
recommendations.notes: May prevent future complications

recommendations.recommendation: In symptomatic patients with moderate or greater PR resulting from treated isolated pulmonary stenosis, with RV dilation or RV dysfunction, pulmonary valve replacement is recommended
recommendations.notes: For post-repair pulmonary regurgitation

recommendations.recommendation: CMR is useful to quantify ventricular size and function, pulmonary valve function, and pulmonary artery anatomy in patients with repaired TOF
recommendations.notes: Gold standard imaging modality

recommendations.recommendation: Coronary artery compression testing is indicated before RV-to-PA conduit stenting or transcatheter valve placement in repaired TOF
recommendations.notes: Essential safety measure

recommendations.recommendation: Pulmonary valve replacement (surgical or percutaneous) for relief of symptoms is recommended for patients with repaired TOF and moderate or greater PR with cardiovascular symptoms not otherwise explained
recommendations.notes: Symptomatic patients benefit from intervention

recommendations.recommendation: Primary prevention ICD therapy is reasonable in adults with TOF and multiple risk factors for SCD
recommendations.notes: Risk stratification remains imperfect

recommendations.recommendation: Ambulatory monitoring for bradycardia or sinus node dysfunction is recommended for adults with d-TGA with atrial switch, especially if treated with beta blockers or other rate-slowing agents
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Adults with d-TGA with atrial switch repair should undergo annual imaging with either echocardiography or CMR to evaluate for common long-term complications
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: GDMT with appropriate attention to the need for anticoagulation is recommended to promptly restore sinus rhythm for adults with d-TGA with atrial switch repair presenting with atrial arrhythmia
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation

recommendations.recommendation: Tricuspid valve replacement is recommended for symptomatic adults with CCTGA and severe TR, and preserved or mildly depressed systemic ventricular function
recommendations.notes: Class I recommendation for CCTGA

medications.indication: History of gout in cyanotic patients
Arrhythmia treatment, systemic RV with pacemaker
Treatment of hypertension in patients with coarctation of the aorta
Atrial arrhythmias in d-TGA with atrial switch to prevent thromboembolism
Maintenance of sinus rhythm in atrial switch patients

red_flags: Signs of heart failure in ACHD patients
Arrhythmias in complex congenital heart disease
Endocarditis risk in patients with congenital heart defects
Complete heart block development in CCTGA or post-AVSD repair patients
New or worsening congestion or low cardiac output syndrome
Sinus node dysfunction in atrial switch patients
New headache or neurologic signs in cyanotic patients (assess for cerebral abscess, hemorrhage, stroke)
PA systolic pressure >50% of systemic pressure
Right-to-left shunting or cyanosis at rest
Myocardial ischemia or stroke in iron-deficient cyanotic patients
Signs of multiorgan dysfunction in cyanotic patients
Eisenmenger syndrome with net right-to-left shunting
Complete heart block (may occur up to 15 years post-surgery)
New onset arrhythmias or conduction abnormalities
Symptoms of coronary ischemia in Williams syndrome patients
Rapid aortic dilation in Turner syndrome
Exercise intolerance with severe valve stenosis
Heart failure symptoms with valve obstruction
High-risk pathway conduction in Ebstein anomaly
Ventricular preexcitation with multiple accessory pathways
Systemic desaturation and arrhythmias indicating worsening hemodynamics
Progressive tricuspid regurgitation or worsening RV function
Heart failure or RV systolic dysfunction development
Sudden cardiac death risk factors: nonsustained VT, QRS duration ≥180ms, extensive RV fibrosis
Stent fracture in conduit patients presenting with worsening PS or PR
Infective endocarditis symptoms: fever, malaise, worsening valve function
Sustained intra-atrial reentrant tachycardia as potential cause of sudden cardiac death
Atrial arrhythmias preceding or coexisting with ventricular tachycardia in 50% of cases
Progressive loss of sinus rhythm in atrial switch patients
Severe tricuspid regurgitation with systemic ventricular dysfunction in CCTGA
extraction.follow_up: Lifelong specialized ACHD care required due to ongoing sequelae that may manifest decades after initial repair
extraction.escalation_path: Refer to specialized ACHD programs for multidisciplinary care including congenital cardiologists, surgeons, and other specialists as needed
